title: Polish pass: 5 LOW findings from TASK-004 review
description:
The Reviewer's audit of TASK-004 (see Reviewer's review output, captured in tasks/TASK-004.review.md if extracted) returned APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS, two MEDIUM findings (fixed inline before closing TASK-004), and five LOW findings deferred to this ticket:

1. **Researcher Write tool grant is unscoped**: `.claude/agents/researcher.md` frontmatter declares `Write` as a tool grant without a path scope, while the body prose says "your only writes are to `.claude/skills/`" and "you never modify `knowledge/` files yourself". The Claude Agent SDK doesn't enforce subpath restrictions. Pre-existing surface; not introduced by TASK-004 but magnified by the new mandatory KB-lookup section. Consider an Orchestrator-side guardrail that rejects researcher tool calls writing outside `.claude/skills/`.

2. **`src/lifecycle.js#loadActiveBundleWithFallback` ambiguous error**: when the pointer is null, falls back to "most recently modified bundle by `session.json` mtime" so `pause` on an already-ended session surfaces `E_INVALID_TRANSITION` instead of `E_NO_ACTIVE_SESSION`. Subtle side effect: if a chat is truly idle (pointer cleared by prior `end`) and user calls `pauseSession` thinking they're starting fresh, code silently targets most-recent ended bundle and rejects with `E_INVALID_TRANSITION` rather than a clearer "no active session" error. Limit fallback to the case where most-recent bundle's `lifecycle_state === 'ended'` and surface a distinct error code for the no-bundle case.

3. **`src/lifecycle.js#endSession` summary.md not atomic**: `summary.md` is written via plain `writeFileSync`, not the atomic helper used everywhere else. Low risk (runs once per session, informational file), but inconsistent with the "all bundle writes are atomic" framing. Route through `atomicWriteFile`.

4. **`src/migrate.js` comment drift**: research §B describes the lift as preserving the v1 payload "verbatim with an added `session_id` field"; the implementation also injects `lifecycle_state: 'active'` and defaults `open_questions`/`blockers`/`decisions`/`subagent_results`/`pending_human_confirmation` if absent. Sensible defaulting, but a one-line comment in `src/migrate.js` documenting the deliberate departure from "verbatim" would help future readers.

5. **`tests/lifecycle.spec.js:174` regex too loose**: assertion `expect(caught.code).toMatch(/^(?:E_LIFECYCLE|E_ENDED_NO_PAUSE|E_INVALID_TRANSITION)/)` accepts three error-code prefixes; implementation emits exactly one (`E_INVALID_TRANSITION`). Tighten to the single code to catch accidental error-code drift.
acceptance_criteria: All 5 LOW items are either implemented or explicitly closed-as-wontfix with rationale in this ticket's comments.
`npm test` continues to report 30/30 passing (or higher count if new tests are added). No regressions in the existing suite.

Each fix that lands has a corresponding test where it makes sense — particularly item #2 (distinct error codes for the no-bundle vs. ended-bundle cases) and item #5 (the tightened regex itself acts as the test).

Item #1 (researcher Write scope) is the largest of the five and may need its own Orchestrator-side change beyond .claude/agents/. If the work scope grows past ~100 lines, split it into a separate ticket and link from here.
